leagueBriefing: Top-ranked Tennessee remains the standard of excellence, surviving an early scare against LSU to maintain its perfect 22-0 record. Elsewhere, offensive fireworks dominated the ACC as Virginia and North Carolina combined for a staggering 33 runs, while pitching clinics from UCLA, Washington, and Texas highlighted a day of elite defensive execution across the top 25.

games.headline: Leach twins spark six-run rally as No. 1 Tennessee stays perfect
games.brief: Top-ranked Tennessee showed their championship mettle by erasing an early 4-0 deficit against No. 17 LSU. The Lady Vols' offense erupted for six runs in the third inning to take control, eventually clinching the SEC opening series in front of a raucous home crowd.
bullets: Gabby Leach finished a perfect 4-for-4 with two home runs.
The Leach twins, Alannah and Gabby, hit consecutive home runs in the sixth inning to ice the game.
Sophomore Maddi Rutan earned the win in relief and delivered a crucial go-ahead two-RBI double.
Tennessee moves to 22-0, extending the best start in program history.
games.context: Tennessee solidifies its No. 1 ranking by clinching its first SEC series of the season.

games.headline: Gutierrez and Kavan combine for four-hit shutout of Gamecocks
games.brief: No. 3 Texas relied on its elite pitching staff to stifle No. 24 South Carolina, securing a series-clinching shutout in Columbia. After five scoreless innings, the Longhorns' bats finally woke up in the sixth to provide the run support needed for Citlaly Gutierrez.
bullets: Citlaly Gutierrez and Teagan Kavan combined for a four-hit shutout.
Leighann Goode broke the 0-0 tie with an RBI double in the top of the sixth.
Hannah Wells provided insurance with a two-RBI single in the seventh.
The win extends Texas' impressive winning streak to 19 games.
games.context: Texas continues to look like a national title contender with dominant SEC road performances.

games.headline: Oklahoma's power surge continues with 10th straight run-rule win
games.brief: The Oklahoma juggernaut showed no signs of slowing down, dismantling Louisiana in a five-inning run-rule victory. The Sooners' lineup launched eight home runs across their Saturday doubleheader, proving they are still the most feared offense in the country.
bullets: Oklahoma recorded its 10th consecutive run-rule victory.
Ella Parker led the charge with two home runs in the contest.
The Sooners' pitching staff allowed zero runs across 11 innings during the day's doubleheader.
OU has now won 11 straight games as they head deeper into Big 12 play.
games.context: The Sooners are peaking at the right time, looking nearly invincible in non-conference tournament play.

games.headline: UCLA crushes Wisconsin behind Suarez and Woolery long balls
games.brief: No. 7 UCLA wasted no time asserting dominance over Wisconsin, using early home runs and a stellar pitching performance from Taylor Tinsley to secure a run-rule win. The Bruins scored four in the first and four in the second to put the game out of reach early.
bullets: Taylor Tinsley tossed a complete-game two-hit shutout.
Ramsey Suarez and Jordan Woolery each launched three-run home runs in the first two innings.
The Bruins reached 20 wins on the season with the five-inning victory.
UCLA's defense was flawless, committing zero errors behind Tinsley.
games.context: UCLA moves to 2-0 in Big Ten play, proving they are the team to beat in their new conference.

games.headline: Bell's grand slam helps Arkansas survive Georgia's comeback bid
games.brief: In a classic SEC seesaw battle, No. 9 Arkansas held off a resilient No. 12 Georgia squad to even their weekend series. A massive first-inning grand slam by Tianna Bell gave the Hogs a lead they would narrowly protect through seven innings of high-intensity softball.
bullets: Tianna Bell's first-inning grand slam provided the winning margin for the Razorbacks.
Georgia fought back with four runs in the second inning to keep the game in doubt.
Arkansas improved to 20-2 on the year with the victory.
The two teams combined for 17 hits in a back-and-forth offensive showcase.
games.context: Arkansas successfully evens the series, setting up a high-stakes rubber match on Sunday.

games.headline: Virginia outlasts Tar Heels in 33-run extra-inning marathon
games.brief: In one of the most chaotic games of the season, Virginia outslugged North Carolina in a 33-run affair that required extra innings to decide. The Cavaliers eventually pulled away in the eighth, capping off a game that featured 32 combined hits and multiple lead changes.
bullets: The teams combined for an ACC season-high 33 runs.
Virginia exploded for five runs in the top of the eighth to secure the win.
UNC committed four errors which proved costly in the late-inning collapse.
Virginia moves to 22-2 on the season after the wild offensive display.
games.context: This marathon win cements Virginia's status as a high-octane offensive threat in the ACC.

games.headline: Wang's grand slam carries Cowgirls past UCF
games.brief: No. 12 Oklahoma State leaned on its veteran stars to secure a gritty win over UCF. Caroline Wang's power at the plate and Lexi Kilfoyl's consistency in the circle proved to be too much for the Knights in a closely contested Big 12 matchup.
bullets: Caroline Wang hit two home runs, including a massive grand slam to drive in five runs.
Lexi Kilfoyl struck out seven in a masterful complete-game performance.
UCF's pitching kept it close early, but the Cowgirls broke through in the third.
Oklahoma State improves to 17-6 on the season.
games.context: Oklahoma State keeps pace with the top of the Big 12 standings with the hard-fought victory.

games.headline: Senini sets stolen base record as Nevada upsets No. 18 Oregon
games.brief: Nevada pulled off one of the biggest upsets of the weekend, taking down No. 18 Oregon in Eugene. The day was made even sweeter for the Wolf Pack as Chelie Senini officially became the program's all-time leader in stolen bases during the signature win.
bullets: Chelie Senini recorded her 100th career steal to become Nevada's all-time leader.
Nevada capitalized on two Oregon errors to build an early lead.
The Wolf Pack scored three runs in both the third and fifth innings.
Oregon's late-inning rally fell short as they struggled to find clutch hits.
games.context: Nevada scores a massive resume-building win, while Oregon continues to struggle with consistency.

games.headline: Meylan dominant as No. 22 Washington blanks Northwestern
games.brief: Ruby Meylan was the story in Seattle, tossing a gem to lead No. 22 Washington past Northwestern. In a pitcher's duel where every hit mattered, Meylan's ability to strand runners ensured the Huskies would walk away with a narrow victory.
bullets: Ruby Meylan threw a complete-game shutout with nine strikeouts.
The Huskies offense manufactured single runs in the first and sixth innings.
Northwestern's Ashley Miller took the hard-luck loss despite allowing only three hits.
Washington improved to 18-6 overall with the win.
games.context: Washington maintains its momentum in Big Ten play behind elite Friday/Saturday pitching.

games.headline: Grand Canyon battles through doubleheader to reach 27-0
games.brief: The Grand Canyon Lopes remain the talk of the mid-major world, pushing their perfect start to 27 games. It wasn't easy, as they had to survive a one-run nail-biter against Notre Dame before finding their offensive groove late in the day against Princeton.
bullets: GCU is now 27-0, the best start in program history.
The Lopes overcame a late deficit against Notre Dame to win 5-4.
Against Princeton, the GCU bats racked up 12 hits in a 7-4 victory.
The GCU pitching staff has now held 22 of 27 opponents to 4 runs or fewer.
games.context: GCU is building a legitimate case for a top-16 national seed if they can keep this streak alive.
